rationale: Both reach the correct top-level verdict (close as duplicate of #4808, tracked in #4809), but X is honest about what it could verify — it explicitly flags that #4808 shows closed/unmerged and asks for confirmation the fix actually landed, plus finds a real-looking regression-test coverage gap. Y instead confidently asserts 'already fixed on main' with fabricated-looking exact line numbers and code/comment text it couldn't have actually verified, and its claim that #4810 (not #4808) delivered the fix contradicts the specific duplicate target in the ground truth. X's grounding and calibrated uncertainty make it clearly more trustworthy and useful to a maintainer than Y's overconfident, unverifiable specifics.
